scenarios.name: Submitting with both fields empty is rejected
scenarios.expected: Login is rejected and the user stays on the login screen
steps: [OK] Tap Log in with both fields empty
[OK] Confirm still on the login screen
[OK] Confirm the login screen heading is shown
[OK] Confirm we did not log in

scenarios.name: Submitting with only an email is rejected
scenarios.expected: Login is rejected and the user stays on the login screen
steps: [OK] Type an email, leave password empty
[OK] Tap Log in with only an email entered
[OK] Confirm still on the login screen
[OK] Confirm we did not log in

scenarios.name: Submitting with only a password is rejected
scenarios.expected: Login is rejected and the user stays on the login screen
steps: [OK] Type a password, leave email empty
[OK] Tap Log in with only a password entered
[OK] Confirm still on the login screen
[OK] Confirm we did not log in

scenarios.name: Fields remain usable after the rejected attempts
scenarios.expected: Both fields still accept and display new input
scenarios.actual: Failed at "Tap the email field"  -  Element not found: Text matching regex: name@example.com

steps.name: Tap Log in with both fields empty
Confirm still on the login screen
Confirm the login screen heading is shown
Confirm we did not log in
Type an email, leave password empty
Tap Log in with only an email entered
Confirm still on the login screen
Confirm we did not log in
Type a password, leave email empty
Tap Log in with only a password entered
Confirm still on the login screen
Confirm we did not log in
Tap the email field
steps.error: Element not found: Text matching regex: name@example.com

scenarios.name: App opens with an empty, editable email field
scenarios.expected: App launches to the login screen and the email field can be cleared

scenarios.name: Valid email is typed into the field
scenarios.expected: The typed email address appears exactly as entered
steps: [OK] Confirm the typed email is shown

scenarios.name: Clearing the field restores the placeholder
scenarios.expected: Placeholder text reappears and the previously typed email is no longer shown
steps: [OK] Confirm the old email is gone

scenarios.name: Email with special characters (+tag, subdomain) is accepted
scenarios.expected: The special-character email address is accepted and displayed
steps: [OK] Type an email with a plus-tag and subdomain
[OK] Confirm the special-character email is shown

scenarios.name: Very long email address is accepted
scenarios.expected: A long but valid email is accepted and displayed without truncation or error
steps: [OK] Type a very long but valid email address
[OK] Confirm the long email is shown

scenarios.name: Field stays editable after repeated use
scenarios.expected: The field accepts a new email after multiple prior edits
steps: [OK] Type one more email to confirm the field still accepts input
[OK] Confirm the final email is shown

scenarios.name: Field returns to its empty state at the end
scenarios.expected: Placeholder text is shown again after the final clear
steps: [OK] Confirm the placeholder text is restored at the end

steps.name: Confirm the typed email is shown
Confirm the old email is gone
Type an email with a plus-tag and subdomain
Confirm the special-character email is shown
Type a very long but valid email address
Confirm the long email is shown
Type one more email to confirm the field still accepts input
Confirm the final email is shown
Confirm the placeholder text is restored at the end
